title: Hold action only changes local UI state and does not place the SIP call on hold
reasoning: holdCall is returned as a public hook operation and exposes a held state, but the implementation only flips React state. The remote party and SIP server continue the established media session normally, so users see a held call while audio is not actually held.
reproduction: Establish a SIP call, call holdCall(), and observe that callState becomes held without any SIP re-INVITE or SDP direction change being sent.
recommendation: Implement SIP hold/unhold via re-INVITE/SDP renegotiation before setting the UI to held, or remove/disable the hold control until real hold is supported.
whyTestsDoNotAlreadyCoverThis: No tests are included, and there is no assertion that holdCall sends SIP renegotiation before updating state.
suggestedRegressionTest: Add a hook-level test with a mocked established session that asserts holdCall invokes the SIP re-INVITE/renegotiation API and only transitions to held after success.
minimumFixScope: Update src/hooks/useSip.js holdCall to perform real SIP hold/unhold or stop exposing a misleading held state.
